reasoning: pevar-ugent is 80 (since 1+80 ÷ 9 = 9), and 80 ÷ 5 = 16; 16 is c’hwezek (confirmed by c’hwezek × c’hwec’h = c’hwezek ha pevar-ugent = 96).
hanter kant is 50 (from 100 ÷ 2), so pemp ha hanter kant = 55; 55 − 12 (daouzek) = 43, which is tri ha daou-ugent.
nav warn ugent is 29; tri ha daou-ugent is 43; 43 − 29 = 14, which is pevarzek (since 2 × 7 = pevarzek).
We need 100 ÷ x = 5, so x = 20; 20 is ugent (from tri-ugent = 60 and pevar-ugent = 80).
daou means two; Breton uses the ‘two’ form for paired body parts. Thus ‘two-eyes’ = one person’s eyes.
tri is 3 and c’hwec’h is 6; triwec’h is the irregular ‘three-sixes’, i.e., 3 × 6 = 18.
